stimulus: Mechanical typewriters dominated office work for most of the twentieth ______ these once-essential machines have largely been replaced by computers and word processors.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The convention being tested is the joining of two independent clauses. A comma plus the coordinating conjunction "but" correctly joins the first independent clause ("Mechanical typewriters... century") to the second ("these once-essential machines... word processors").

Choice A creates a run-on sentence because the two independent clauses are fused without punctuation or a conjunction. Choice B creates a comma splice (a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses). Choice D produces an ungrammatical construction with "century that" attempting to subordinate the second clause.
